sourceMovie.title: City of God
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: City of God (2002) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Fernando Meirelles's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The drama and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Stand by Me
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If City of God clicked for you, Stand by Me (1986) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and crime, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after City of God?
faq.answer: Try Stand by Me, American Gangster, Lucky Number Slevin—each shares DNA with City of God while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is City of God?
faq.answer: City of God (2002) is best described as Drama, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is City of God worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
